Bio_About: The first one. I named every creature that breathes, and I tend the ground we were given. Married to Eve. Still learning what it costs to be first at everything.
Family_Links: CHAR_003, CHAR_004, CHAR_005, CHAR_1374, CHAR_1375, CHAR_1376, CHAR_1377, CHAR_1378, CHAR_1379
Personality: Reflective and a little self-deprecating; carries his guilt but leans hard on hope; gently teasing with Eve.

Bio_About: Mother of all the living, or so I'm told I'll be. Married to Adam, who is far sweeter than he lets on. I laugh easily and I hope harder than is probably wise.
Family_Links: CHAR_003, CHAR_004, CHAR_005, CHAR_1374, CHAR_1375, CHAR_1376, CHAR_1377, CHAR_1378, CHAR_1379
Personality: Warm but quick-witted and sarcastic; teases Adam affectionately and never lets his lines slide; fiercely hopeful and maternal.

Bio_About: I work the ground. I bring what my own two hands have grown. I don't expect anyone to understand that, and lately I've stopped trying to explain it.
Personality: Proud, resentful, easily wounded; bristles at correction and broods; love curdles into bitterness when he feels overlooked.

Bio_About: Keeper of sheep. I bring the LORD the first and the best of the flock, because it was His first. Happiest out in the field at first light.
Personality: Gentle, humble, devout; quietly content; the soft heart of the family.

Bio_About: Born after sorrow, named for hope. My mother says I'm a gift given in place of what we lost. I try my best to live like she's right.
Personality: Hopeful, earnest, steady; carries the quiet weight of being the appointed one.

Bio_About: At this time men began to call upon the name of the Lord.
Walking with God every single day. The journey is better than the destination.
Seen many years. Grandfather to Noah. The days are getting darker, but family stays strong.
Just a righteous man in a wicked generation. Currently building a very large boat. Trusting the process.
Standing by Noah. Gathering supplies and keeping the family together for the days ahead.
Working on the ark with family.
Pitching the inside and outside of the ark.
Gathering food for the animals. So many animals.
Building the very first city. Named the city after myself.
Living in the city of Enoch.
Descendant of Cain. Keeping the family line going.
Father to Lamech.
Avenged seventy-sevenfold. Do not cross the family of Lamech.

Bio_About: Wife of Lamech. Mother to Jabal and Jubal.
Wife of Lamech. Mother to Tubal-Cain and Naamah.
Living in tents and raising livestock. It is a peaceful life.
Inventing new ways to make music. Playing the lyre and pipe.
Forging tools out of bronze and iron. Progress.
Sister to Tubal-Cain.
Son of Enosh.
Son of Kenan.
Father of Enoch.
Looking for rest from work and the toil of our hands. Hoping Noah brings comfort.
Helping the family prepare the ark.
Packing and preparing for the flood.
Assisting with the animals and ark provisions.
Descendant of Japheth. Heading north to settle the lands.
Descendant of Japheth. Settling the far north.
Descendant of Japheth. Establishing the coastlands.
Son of Ham. Father of Nimrod.
Son of Ham. Settling the lands of Egypt.
Son of Ham.
Son of Ham. The land ahead is ours.
A mighty hunter before the Lord. Building the first great kingdoms: Babel, Uruk, Akkad, and Nineveh.
Son of Shem. Born two years after the flood.
Son of Arphaxad.
Father of Peleg and Joktan. The Hebrews trace their name to me.
In my days, the earth was divided. The languages have been confused at Babel.
Brother of Peleg. Father of 13 sons settling the eastern hills.
Son of Peleg.
Son of Reu.
Son of Serug. Grandfather to Abram.
Living in Ur of the Chaldeans. Father of Abram, Nahor, and Haran. Time to move to Canaan, but we might settle in Haran first.
Called to leave my country and my father's house. God promised to make me a great nation.
Brother to Abram. Married to Milcah.
Died in the presence of my father Terah in the land of my kindred.

Son of Haran, traveling with my uncle Abram. Looking for well-watered land for my flocks.
Wife of Nahor. Daughter of Haran.
Daughter of Haran.

Maidservant from Egypt. Life took a hard turn, but God saw me by the spring in the wilderness. He is the God who sees. 🌅
Son of Abraham. God promised to make me a great nation too. Living free in the open desert with a bow in hand. 🏹
Steward of Abraham's house. If it must be trusted, trust it to me. Off to find a wife for the young master.
King of Salem. Priest of God Most High. I bring out bread and wine and bless those who fight the good fight.
King of Sodom. The valley is rich and the cities are proud.
King of Gomorrah. Standing with the cities of the plain.
King of Elam. Twelve years they served me; the thirteenth they rebelled. I have come to collect what I am owed.
King of Shinar. Marching with the eastern alliance.
King of Ellasar. Riding with the four kings of the east.
King of Goiim. The nations of the east move as one.
King of Gerar. I act with a clear conscience and clean hands. Let us swear a treaty and keep the peace.
Commander of Gerar's army. I go where the king goes.
Child of the promise, laughter is in my very name. Walking the land my father was given and trusting the God who provides. 🐑
Of the house of Nahor in Paddan-Aram. This matter has come from the LORD; who am I to speak against it?
Drew water for a stranger and all his camels, and it changed my whole life. Veiled and on the road to meet my husband. ✨
Of Haran. Family is family, and a good deal is a good deal. My sister marries well.
Wife of Abraham in his later years. Building a household and raising sons for the eastern lands.

Son of Abraham and Keturah.
Son of Abraham and Keturah. Founding the Midianite people across the eastern wilderness.
Son of Abraham and Keturah.
Son of Abraham and Keturah.
Son of Lot. Father of the Moabites, east of the Salt Sea.
Son of Lot. Father of the Ammonites.
Daughter of Lot. We fled the fire of Sodom and lost everything but our father.
Daughter of Lot. We escaped the cities of the plain with only the clothes we wore.
Firstborn of Ishmael. A prince of the desert tribes.
Son of Ishmael. Tents and flocks across the wilderness.
Son of Ishmael. A prince among the twelve.

Amorite of Hebron. Ally and friend to Abraham. When he needed swords, we rode with him.
Amorite of Hebron. Confederate of Abraham, brother of Mamre.
Amorite of Hebron. Confederate of Abraham.
Hittite of Hebron. Sold Abraham the field and the cave of Machpelah for four hundred shekels, a burying place for his dead.
Hittite of Hebron. Father of Ephron.
Wife of Lot. Leaving Sodom behind, though my heart keeps looking back.
Firstborn of Isaac. A man of the open field, give me the hunt and the red hills. That stew? Worth it at the time. 🏹
Family_Links: CHAR_125, CHAR_127, CHAR_171, CHAR_194, CHAR_195, CHAR_196

Bio_About: Younger twin with a strong grip, they call me the heel-grabber. Saw a ladder to heaven once. Still chasing the blessing. 🪜

Bio_About: Eldest daughter of Laban, the one Jacob did not choose. But the LORD has seen me, and He fills my arms with sons. 💧
Shepherdess of Haran. Jacob worked seven years for me, and to him they seemed but a few days for the love he had. 💍
Rachel's maidservant, given to Jacob. Raising sons on behalf of my mistress.
Leah's maidservant, given to Jacob. Adding to the house of Israel.
Firstborn of Jacob, eager, but unsteady as water. Found mandrakes in the field for my mother.
Second son of Leah. Quick to draw the sword when my sister's honor is on the line.
Third son of Leah. Zealous for the family name, sometimes too zealous.
Fourth son of Leah, and this time, I will praise the LORD. Learning my way, for better and for worse. 🦁
Family_Links: CHAR_171, CHAR_172, CHAR_227, CHAR_229, CHAR_230, CHAR_231, CHAR_232, CHAR_233, CHAR_234

Bio_About: Son of Bilhah. God has judged, and judged in my favor.
Son of Bilhah. I have wrestled hard and prevailed. Swift as a doe set loose.
Son of Zilpah. Good fortune has come to me, a raiding band, but I raid at their heels.
Son of Zilpah. Happy am I, the daughters will call me blessed. Bringing royal delicacies. 🫒
Son of Leah. A strong donkey of a man, content between the sheepfolds and the good land.
Son of Leah. Now my father will honor my mother, six sons she has given him. Dwelling toward the shore.
Daughter of Leah and Jacob. I went out to see the women of the land, and everything changed. 💔
Son of Rachel, the dreamer of the family. Had the strangest dream last night about sheaves and stars... ✨
Family_Links: CHAR_171, CHAR_173, CHAR_188, CHAR_222, CHAR_224, CHAR_225

Bio_About: Youngest of the twelve. My mother's last breath named me son of sorrow, but my father calls me son of his right hand.
Nurse to Rebekah since the days of Haran. I have held this family together for two generations.
Hivite, prince of Shechem. Let us live together, trade together, and join our families. The land is wide enough for us all.
Son of Hamor, prince of the city. My heart is set on Jacob's daughter, name any bride-price and it is paid.
Daughter of Beeri the Hittite. A wife of Esau.
Daughter of Elon the Hittite. A wife of Esau.
Daughter of Ishmael, sister of Nebaioth. A wife of Esau, joining the houses of Abraham's sons.
Daughter of Elon the Hittite. Wife of Esau and mother of Eliphaz.
Daughter of Anah, of the line of Seir. Wife of Esau and mother of chiefs in Edom.
Hittite of Canaan. Father of Judith.
Hittite of Canaan. Father of Esau's wives.
Of the Horite line of Seir. Father of Oholibamah. Found the hot springs in the wilderness while pasturing the donkeys.
Hivite of Seir, of the Horite chiefs. Father of Anah.
Firstborn of Esau by Adah. Chief of the Edomite clans of Teman.
Family_Links: CHAR_170, CHAR_195, CHAR_206, CHAR_207, CHAR_208, CHAR_209, CHAR_210, CHAR_212

Bio_About: Son of Esau by Basemath. A chief among the clans of Edom.
Family_Links: CHAR_170, CHAR_194, CHAR_213, CHAR_214, CHAR_215, CHAR_216

Bio_About: Son of Esau by Oholibamah. A chief of Edom.
Son of Esau by Oholibamah. A chief of Edom.
Son of Esau by Oholibamah. A chief of Edom.
Son of Eliphaz. The clan of Teman is known for its wise and its mighty men.
Son of Eliphaz. A chief of Edom.
Son of Eliphaz. A chief of Edom.
Son of Eliphaz. A chief of Edom.
Son of Eliphaz. A chief of Edom.
Concubine of Eliphaz, of the Horite people of Seir. Mother of Amalek.
Son of Eliphaz by Timna. My people will roam the southern desert, and the world will not forget the name Amalek.
Son of Reuel. A chief of Edom.
Son of Reuel. A chief of Edom.
Son of Reuel. A chief of Edom.
Son of Reuel. A chief of Edom.
Captain of Pharaoh's guard, officer of Egypt. I know a trustworthy man when I see one, gave him my whole house. Until I didn't.
Lady of a great Egyptian house. Used to getting what I want, and I had my eye on my husband's handsome steward.
Pharaoh, lord of the Two Lands, the living Horus. I dreamed of seven fat cows and seven lean. Who can tell me what it means? 🐄
I place the cup in Pharaoh's hand. Had a dream of three vines once, a young Hebrew in the prison read it true. I should have remembered him sooner.
I bake for Pharaoh's table. Three baskets of bread in my dream... I hope it ends as well for me as for the cupbearer.
Daughter of the priest of On. Wife of the vizier who reads dreams and saved Egypt. Mother to two sons in this new land.
Priest of On, servant of the sun. I gave my daughter to Pharaoh's vizier, the Hebrew who fills the storehouses.
Firstborn of Joseph. God has made me forget all my hardship and my father's house. Born in the land of Egypt.
Second son of Joseph. God has made me fruitful in the land of my affliction. Grandfather Israel crossed his hands and blessed me first. 🌾
Adullamite, friend of Judah. We go down to shear the sheep and toast the harvest together.
Daughter of Shua the Canaanite, wife of Judah. Mother of his three sons.
Canaanite of Adullam. Father-in-law to Judah.
Firstborn of Judah, wed to Tamar.
Second son of Judah.
Youngest son of Judah by Bath-shua.
Widow of Judah's house, wronged and made to wait. I will secure my rightful place in this family, one way or another.
Son of Judah and Tamar, I broke through first! A breach made for me. (They say a line of kings will run through this house.)
Son of Judah and Tamar. My hand came out first, with the scarlet thread tied round it.
King of Egypt, and I know nothing of any Joseph. These Hebrews breed too fast. Hard labor for the men, the river for their sons.
Hebrew midwife. The king commands one thing; God commands another. I fear God, so the baby boys live.
Hebrew midwife, alongside Shiphrah. We told Pharaoh the Hebrew women are just too quick for us. 😇 (The babies are perfectly fine.)
Levite in Egypt. My wife and I hid our son for three months, then trusted him to the river and to God.
Daughter of Levi, mother of Moses, Aaron, and Miriam. I made a basket of reeds and gave my baby to the Nile, and the LORD gave him back to me to nurse. 🧺
Sister of Moses and Aaron. I watched over my baby brother among the reeds, and I'll lead the women with timbrels when we cross to freedom. 🥁
Brother of Moses, and the one who speaks for him. Levite, and soon the first high priest of Israel. The words come easier to me than to him.
Family_Links: CHAR_238, CHAR_239, CHAR_240, CHAR_242, CHAR_252, CHAR_254, CHAR_255, CHAR_256, CHAR_257

Bio_About: Drawn from the water, raised in a palace, exiled to the desert. Now the LORD speaks to me from a bush that burns but is not consumed. Who am I to go to Pharaoh? 🔥
Family_Links: CHAR_238, CHAR_239, CHAR_240, CHAR_241, CHAR_245, CHAR_246, CHAR_247

Bio_About: Daughter of Pharaoh. I went down to bathe at the river and found a crying baby in a basket. I'll call him Moses, for I drew him out of the water.
Priest of Midian. Gave my daughter to a runaway from Egypt, he herds my flocks now, but I suspect the LORD has far bigger plans for this one.
Daughter of Jethro of Midian, wife of Moses. I've followed this man from the desert toward a destiny I don't fully understand yet. 🐑
Firstborn of Moses and Zipporah. My name means 'a stranger there', for my father was a sojourner in a foreign land.
Second son of Moses. My name means 'God is my help', He delivered my father from the sword of Pharaoh.
Pharaoh, the living god of Egypt. Who is the LORD, that I should obey His voice? I will not let these people go. (My heart only hardens with every plague.) 🐸🩸
Magician of Pharaoh's court. My staff becomes a serpent too, though Aaron's swallowed mine. We can match the first wonders... for a while.
Sorcerer of Egypt, alongside Jannes. We turned water to blood and called up frogs, but the gnats? Even we said: this is the finger of God.
Of the tribe of Judah. Father of Nahshon, and of Elisheba who married Aaron the priest.
Daughter of Amminadab, sister of Nahshon. Wife of Aaron and mother of the first priestly sons of Israel.
Family_Links: CHAR_251, CHAR_253, CHAR_241, CHAR_254, CHAR_255, CHAR_256, CHAR_257

Bio_About: Prince of the tribe of Judah, son of Amminadab. Leader of my people through the wilderness. (They say a royal line will rise from my house.) 👑
Eldest son of Aaron, anointed to serve before the LORD as a priest of Israel.
Son of Aaron, priest of Israel. My brother Nadab and I serve together at the altar.
Son of Aaron, a priest of Israel, and the line of high priests will continue through me.
Family_Links: CHAR_241, CHAR_252, CHAR_254, CHAR_255, CHAR_257, CHAR_258

Bio_About: Youngest son of Aaron, priest of Israel, charged with the care of the tabernacle and its furnishings.
Son of Eleazar, grandson of Aaron. Zealous for the LORD and for the holiness of His people.
Of the tribe of Judah. When Moses' arms grew heavy on the hilltop, Aaron and I held them up until the sun went down. Standing with Moses.
Son of Nun, of the tribe of Ephraim. Moses' aide and Israel's commander in battle. Where he leads, I go, and I do not leave the tent of meeting. ⚔️
Son of Uri, grandson of Hur, of Judah. Filled with the Spirit of God in wisdom and craft, I'll build the tabernacle, the ark, all of it, exactly to His design. 🛠️
Son of Ahisamach, of the tribe of Dan. Engraver, weaver, and craftsman, appointed to help Bezalel build the dwelling place of God.
Of the tribe of Dan, daughter of Dibri. My son's quarrel in the camp ended in sorrow and judgment.
Prince of the tribe of Reuben, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Simeon, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Issachar, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Zebulun, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Ephraim, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Manasseh, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Benjamin, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Dan, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Asher, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Gad, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Prince of the tribe of Naphtali, chosen to lead my people through the wilderness and to bring my tribe's offering before the LORD.
Of Judah, son of Jephunneh. I scouted the land and saw giants, but I saw that our God is bigger. Let us go up at once and take it! Give me that mountain. 💪
Levite, son of Izhar of the Kohathites. Why should Moses and Aaron lift themselves above us? The whole congregation is holy, every one of them.
Reubenite, son of Eliab. We followed Moses out of Egypt for this, to die in a wilderness? I will not come up when he summons.
Reubenite, brother of Dathan. Standing against Moses and against his brother's grip on the priesthood.
Reubenite, son of Peleth. I joined the grumbling against Moses... though my wife has some very strong opinions about that. 😬
One of the seventy elders. The Spirit rested on me and I prophesied, right in the camp, not at the tent. Moses said he wished all the LORD's people were prophets!
Elder of Israel. Eldad and I prophesied in the camp by the Spirit of God. Let it fall where it will.
Seer of Pethor by the River. Kings send for me to bless and to curse, but I can only speak what the LORD puts in my mouth. (Some days my donkey sees more than I do.) 🫏
King of Moab. These Israelites cover the face of the land like locusts. Balaam, come curse this people for me, and name your price.
A leader in the tribe of Simeon, son of Salu. I brought a Midianite woman into the camp in broad daylight, and it cost me everything.
Daughter of Zur, a chief of Midian. My name is on the lips of all Israel now, for all the wrong reasons.
A chief of Midian, head of a people. Father of Cozbi.
King of the Amorites at Heshbon. I would not let Israel pass through my land, so I marched my army out against them.
King of Bashan, last of the Rephaim. They say my iron bedstead measures nine cubits. I feared no one... until Israel reached my border.
Of the tribe of Manasseh. I died in the wilderness with no sons, only five daughters, who will make certain my name is never forgotten.
Daughter of Zelophehad, of Manasseh. My sisters and I stood before Moses: why should our father's name die out for want of a son? Give us our inheritance.
Daughter of Zelophehad, of Manasseh. My sisters and I stood before Moses: why should our father's name die out for want of a son? Give us our inheritance.
Daughter of Zelophehad, of Manasseh. My sisters and I stood before Moses: why should our father's name die out for want of a son? Give us our inheritance.
Daughter of Zelophehad, of Manasseh. My sisters and I stood before Moses: why should our father's name die out for want of a son? Give us our inheritance.
Daughter of Zelophehad, of Manasseh. My sisters and I stood before Moses: why should our father's name die out for want of a son? Give us our inheritance.
Of Jericho. I hid two Israelite spies on my roof under the flax, because I've heard what their God did at the Red Sea, and I believe Him. Tie the scarlet cord in my window and spare my family. 🧵
Of the tribe of Judah, son of Carmi. Among the spoils of Jericho I saw a beautiful robe, silver, and gold, and I took them, and buried them under my tent. God forgive me for what I've cost Israel.
King of Jerusalem. Gibeon has made peace with these invaders, this cannot stand. Kings of the Amorites, march with me and we will crush them together!
King of Hebron. Marching with the five kings against Gibeon.
King of Jarmuth. Joined to the Amorite coalition against Israel.
King of Lachish. One of the five kings who marched against Gibeon.
King of Eglon. One of the five Amorite kings who hid in the cave at Makkedah.
King of Hazor, head of the kings of the north. I have summoned every army of the north, horses and chariots like the sand on the seashore, to stop Israel cold.
Of Judah, son of Kenaz, kinsman of Caleb. I took Kiriath-sepher, and won Achsah's hand in the bargain. (One day Israel will call on me to lead.) ⚔️
Daughter of Caleb. My father gave me in marriage with a field, so I asked him for the springs of water too, the upper and the lower. It never hurts to ask. 💧
King of Aram-Naharaim. For eight years Israel served me, until their God raised up a deliverer named Othniel.
Of Benjamin, son of Gera, and left-handed, which Eglon never saw coming. I had a message from God for the king... on an eighteen-inch blade. 🗡️
King of Moab, and yes, a very large man. Israel has served me eighteen years. That Benjamite says he has a secret word for me alone... send everyone out.
Son of Anath. No army and no sword, just an ox goad and the LORD's help. Six hundred Philistines later, Israel could breathe again.
Prophetess and judge of Israel, they come to me under the palm to settle their disputes. I told Barak: the LORD has gone out before you. Arise! 🌴
Son of Abinoam, of Naphtali. Ten thousand men at my back, but I will not march on Sisera unless Deborah goes with me. Some battles you don't face alone.
Commander of Jabin's army, nine hundred chariots of iron under my hand, and Israel has never had an answer for them. (Though the river Kishon is rising...)
Wife of Heber the Kenite. Sisera fled to my tent for shelter. I gave him milk, a blanket... and while he slept, a tent peg and a hammer. Most blessed of women, they'll sing. 🔨
Of the Kenites, descended from Moses' father-in-law. I pitched my tent apart from my kin, near the oak at Kedesh.
King of Canaan, reigning in Hazor. With Sisera as my commander and his iron chariots, I held Israel under my heel for twenty years.

Son of Joash the Abiezrite. The angel found me threshing wheat in a winepress, hiding from Midian, and called me 'mighty warrior.' Me? I asked for a sign with the fleece... twice. 🐑 (They also call me Jerubbaal.)
The Abiezrite of Ophrah, father of Gideon. When the town wanted my son dead for tearing down Baal's altar, I said: if Baal is a god, let him contend for himself.
A prince of Midian. Israel's sudden rout has run us all the way to the rock that now bears my name.
A prince of Midian, with Oreb. The winepress where I fell will carry my name.
King of Midian, fleeing Gideon's three hundred. We slew his brothers at Tabor, and he has not forgotten it.
King of Midian, with Zebah. We taunted Gideon to strike us himself, his boy's hand trembled too much for it.
Son of Gideon by his Shechemite concubine. I made myself king, and to clear the throne I dealt with my seventy brothers upon one stone. Shechem crowned me.
Youngest of Gideon's seventy sons, the only one who escaped Abimelech. From the top of Mount Gerizim I told them a parable of the trees who wanted a king. Listen well.
Son of Ebed. The men of Shechem are tired of Abimelech, and I'll say it out loud: who is Abimelech that we should serve him?
Governor of Shechem under Abimelech. I keep my eyes open and send word to the king when traitors start talking.
Son of Puah, of Issachar. I rose to deliver Israel and judged them twenty-three years from the hills of Ephraim.
The Gileadite. I judged Israel twenty-two years, and my thirty sons rode thirty donkeys and held thirty towns.
Son of Gilead by a prostitute, my brothers cast me out, then came begging when Ammon attacked. I'll lead them. But I made a vow to the LORD I would give anything to keep... and everything to undo.
Only child of Jephthah. My father came home in victory and tore his clothes at the very sight of me. I asked only for two months in the hills with my friends, to weep. 💔
Of Bethlehem. I judged Israel seven years, with thirty sons and thirty daughters all married off far and wide.
The Zebulunite. I judged Israel ten years.
Son of Hillel, of Pirathon. I judged Israel eight years, forty sons and thirty grandsons, riding on seventy donkeys.
Of Dan, from Zorah. An angel told my barren wife we'd have a son, set apart to God from the womb, no razor to touch his head. I just wanted to know how to raise the boy right.
Wife of Manoah. The angel of the LORD came to me first: I am to bear a Nazirite son, a deliverer of Israel. No wine for me, and no razor, ever, for him.
Nazirite of Dan, never cut my hair, never will. Strongest man you'll ever meet (just ask the lion 🦁). I'll admit it: I've got a weakness for Philistine women and a good riddle.
A woman of Timnah. Samson saw me and wanted me, 'she is right in my eyes,' he said. Our wedding feast and his riddle did not end well for anyone.
Of the Valley of Sorek. The lords of the Philistines offered me eleven hundred pieces of silver each to learn the secret of Samson's strength. A girl just has to keep asking... and asking. 💇
Of the hill country of Ephraim. I returned my mother's stolen silver, she had it cast into an idol for our house, and I hired a Levite as my own priest. Surely now the LORD will be good to me.
A young Levite of Bethlehem, looking for a place in the world. Micah took me in as his priest, then the Danites made me a far better offer, for a whole tribe.
Of Bethlehem in Judah. Famine drove us to Moab, where I buried my husband and both my sons. I came home empty, call me Mara, for the Almighty has dealt bitterly with me. (But God is not finished.) 🌾
An Ephrathite of Bethlehem. The famine was severe, so I took my family to the fields of Moab to survive. I never saw home again.
Son of Elimelech and Naomi. I took a Moabite wife, Ruth, a good woman. My health failed me young in a foreign land.
Son of Elimelech and Naomi. I married Orpah of Moab. Like my brother, I did not live to grow old.
A Moabite who married into an Israelite family, then lost my husband. I will not leave Naomi: where you go I will go, your people my people, your God my God. Gleaning in the barley fields now. 🌾
A Moabite, married to Naomi's son Chilion. When we were all widowed, I kissed Naomi goodbye and went back to my mother's house and my people. Ruth chose differently.
Of the tribe of Judah, son of Nahshon. I married Rahab of Jericho, yes, that Rahab, and our son Boaz carries the line on.
A worthy man of Bethlehem with fields of barley and a good name. A Moabite widow has caught my eye, gleaning behind my harvesters, and I mean to do right by her as kinsman-redeemer. 🌾
A kinsman of Elimelech, nearer than Boaz. I'd redeem the land gladly, but take Ruth and risk my own inheritance? No. Let Boaz have it. Here is my sandal.
Son of Boaz and Ruth, born to comfort Naomi in her old age. They say my line will matter someday, my son will be Jesse, and his son... well, we'll see. 👶
Of Ramah in the hills of Ephraim. Two wives, one heartache, I love Hannah, but oh, the strife at home. 'Am I not more to you than ten sons?' I asked her.

Wife of Elkanah and, for years, childless, while Peninnah never let me forget it. I wept at Shiloh and vowed: give me a son, LORD, and I'll give him back to You. His name is Samuel. My heart rejoices in the LORD. 🙏
The other wife of Elkanah. I have children and Hannah has none, and yes, I remind her of it. Not my finest quality, I'll admit.

Priest and judge at Shiloh, keeper of the ark. My eyes grow dim, and so, I fear, does my house, for my sons dishonor the altar and I was too soft to stop them. I taught young Samuel to answer: 'Speak, LORD, your servant hears.'
Son of Eli, priest at Shiloh. My brother and I take the best of the offerings for ourselves. The people are starting to despise the very sacrifice of the LORD.
Son of Eli, priest at Shiloh. We carried the ark into battle to save ourselves, surely God had to back us. (It did not go as we planned.)
Born the day the ark was captured and my father fell. My mother named me with her dying breath: Ichabod, 'the glory has departed from Israel.'
Lent to the LORD before I was weaned. Raised in the house of Eli; the LORD called me in the night. Prophet, priest, and the last judge of Israel, and now the one who must anoint her kings. 🕯️
Firstborn of Samuel, a judge in Beersheba. I'll confess: we did not walk in our father's ways, we took bribes and bent justice.
Son of Samuel, judge in Beersheba beside my brother. Our failures are why Israel began clamoring for a king like the other nations.
A Benjamite of substance, son of Abiel. My donkeys wandered off, so I sent my son Saul to find them. He came home anointed king. Some errand, that.

Son of Kish, a head taller than any man in Israel, and her first king. I started out hiding among the baggage. Lately the Spirit has left me, a dark mood hunts me... and so, I confess, does that boy David. 👑
Family_Links: CHAR_359, CHAR_361, CHAR_362, CHAR_364, CHAR_365, CHAR_366, CHAR_367, CHAR_385, CHAR_390

Bio_About: Daughter of Ahimaaz, wife of King Saul, mother of his children. I watch my husband's mind darken and I fear for us all.
Eldest son of Saul, heir to the throne, and I would give it all for my friend David. The two of us once routed a whole Philistine garrison, just me and my armor-bearer. My soul is knit to his. 🏹
Family_Links: CHAR_360, CHAR_364, CHAR_365, CHAR_366, CHAR_367, CHAR_391

Bio_About: Son of Ner, commander of Saul's army and the king's cousin. Where the king leads, the army follows, and I'll hold this house together as long as I can.
Son of King Saul. I stand with my father and my brothers against the Philistines.
Son of King Saul, brother of Jonathan. Where my father's banner goes, I go.
Elder daughter of King Saul. I was promised to the giant-killer David... then given to another man. Royal promises are slippery things.

Younger daughter of Saul, and I love David. I let him down through the window on a rope and laid an idol in his bed to fool my father's men. A king's daughter and a fugitive's wife: it's complicated. 💔
King of the Ammonites. I offered Jabesh-gilead peace, on the one condition that I gouge out every right eye among them. That is my mercy.
King of the Amalekites. Saul spared me when his God said destroy us all. 'Surely the bitterness of death is past,' I thought. Samuel thought otherwise.
Of Bethlehem, son of Obed, grandson of Ruth and Boaz. Eight sons, and the prophet Samuel came and anointed the youngest, my shepherd boy, while the older seven stood by stunned.

Youngest of Jesse's eight, a shepherd of Bethlehem with a sling and a harp. Anointed by Samuel, I faced a giant with five smooth stones. Now I play the lyre for a king who throws spears at me. The LORD is my shepherd. 🎵
Family_Links: CHAR_370, CHAR_372, CHAR_373, CHAR_374, CHAR_367, CHAR_381, CHAR_382, CHAR_396, CHAR_398, CHAR_393, CHAR_394, CHAR_395, CHAR_397, CHAR_400

Bio_About: Eldest son of Jesse. Tall enough that Samuel thought I'd be the one, but the LORD looks at the heart, not the height. (And no, little brother, you have no business at this battle.)
Second son of Jesse, of Bethlehem. Passed over by Samuel's horn of oil, like all of us but the youngest.
Third son of Jesse. We tend the flocks and march when Israel calls.
Champion of Gath, six cubits and a span of bronze and iron. Forty days I've taunted Israel's army, send me a man! They sent a boy with a stick and a sling. 🪨
King of Gath. A Hebrew outlaw named David came seeking refuge from Saul, feigned madness at my gate the first time. A useful man to have on my side... or so I tell myself.
Chief of Saul's herdsmen, an Edomite. I saw the priest of Nob help David, and when no Israelite would lift a hand, I struck down eighty-five priests at the king's word.
Priest of Nob, of the house of Eli. David came hungry and unarmed; I gave him the holy bread and Goliath's sword. I did not know I was helping a fugitive, and it cost my whole town.
Son of Ahimelech, the only priest of Nob to escape Doeg's slaughter. I fled to David with the ephod, I'll seek the word of the LORD for him now.
A rich man of Maon, flocks in Carmel, and yes, my name means 'fool.' David's men guarded my shearers, then asked for food. Who is this son of Jesse? I gave him nothing.

Wife of Nabal, wise, where he is foolish. When his rudeness nearly brought David's sword on us all, I rode out with bread, wine, and good sense, and made peace. (Heaven help me, I think the future king noticed.) 🍇
Of Jezreel, a wife of David in his wilderness years. I share these hard, wandering days with him and his men.
Son of Zeruiah, David's sister, so, his nephew, and one of his fiercest men. I slipped into Saul's camp at night with David and said: let me pin him to the ground with one stroke. David said no.
A woman of Endor with a familiar spirit, the very kind King Saul outlawed. Then he came to me in disguise, by night, begging me to call up Samuel. What I saw terrified me.
Son of Saul, set on my father's throne by Abner. I reign over Israel from Mahanaim while David holds Judah. A king who jumps at shadows, if I'm honest.

Son of Zeruiah, David's sister, and commander of his army. I do the hard, bloody things the king won't, and I won't apologize for keeping his throne. I killed Abner for my brother; I'll deal with Absalom too. ⚔️
Son of Zeruiah, brother of Joab, swift of foot as a wild gazelle. I chased Abner across the field and would not turn aside. (I should have.)
A Benjamite captain under Ishbosheth, until I judged the wind was changing.
Captain of Ishbosheth's raiders, with my brother Rechab. We thought David would thank us for the king's head. We thought very wrong.

Concubine of Saul, daughter of Aiah. They hanged my sons on the hill, so I spread sackcloth on a rock and guarded their bodies from the birds by day and the beasts by night, until the rains came. A mother does not leave. 🕯️

Son of Jonathan, grandson of Saul. Dropped by my nurse as a child when the news of Gilboa came, lame in both feet ever since. King David showed me kindness for my father's sake, and seats me at his own table. 🍞
Servant of the house of Saul, steward to Mephibosheth. When David fled Absalom, I knew which side my bread was buttered, and brought the king supplies and a useful story.
Firstborn of David, by Ahinoam. I am sick with desire for my half-sister Tamar, and my cousin Jonadab has a clever, terrible plan.

Third son of David, by Maacah, the handsomest man in Israel, and yes, you've heard about the hair. 💇 My sister Tamar was wronged and the king did nothing, so I waited two years and settled it. Now I'll have his kingdom too. Hearts of Israel, come to me.
Fourth son of David, by Haggith, handsome, and next in line now that my brothers are gone. When the king grows old, the crown should be mine. (Solomon and his mother may disagree.)
Daughter of Talmai, king of Geshur, and a wife of David. Mother of Absalom and Tamar, and oh, how I grieve for what became of them both.
Daughter of David, sister of Absalom. My half-brother Amnon shamed me and threw me out, and tore my royal robe of many colors. My brother took me into his house. I am desolate. 💔

Daughter of Eliam, wife of Uriah the Hittite. The king saw me from his roof, and nothing has been the same since. I lost a husband and a baby, but my son Solomon will sit on the throne. I have learned how this court works.
A Hittite, and one of the king's mighty men. While the army camps in the open field and the ark sits in a tent, I will not go home to my own bed and my wife. How could I? My loyalty was my undoing.
Son of David and Bathsheba, the LORD loved me and named me Jedidiah. One day this throne, and a temple, will be mine. For now, I watch and I learn. 👑
Prophet of the LORD in David's court. I told the king a parable about a poor man's one little lamb, then four words he never forgot: 'You are the man.' I speak truth to power, even crowned power.
Seer to David since his fugitive days. When the king sinned in numbering Israel, I brought him the LORD's hard choice of judgments. A prophet's work is rarely a happy one.
David's most trusted counselor, my advice was as the very word of God, they said. But the king wronged my family, so when Absalom rose, I went with him. (My granddaughter, you see, was Bathsheba.)
The Archite, friend of David. I stayed behind in Jerusalem to pose as Absalom's man and wreck Ahithophel's good counsel from the inside. Loyalty sometimes has to wear a mask.
Son of David's sister Abigail, cousin to Joab and Absalom alike. Absalom made me his commander; David forgave me and offered me Joab's post. Then Joab embraced me... with a knife.
Of the house of Saul, son of Gera. When David fled in disgrace, I walked the ridge cursing him and throwing stones and dust. 'Get out, you man of blood!' (I may have spoken too soon.)

A very old, very rich Gileadite of Rogelim. When the king fled across the Jordan, I brought beds, food, and provisions for him and all his men. Too old to follow him to court, but glad to have served.
Son of Zadok the priest. Fast on my feet, I ran ahead with news from the battle, though Joab warned me I carried tidings the king would not want to hear.
Priest of the LORD alongside Abiathar. I kept faith with David through Absalom's revolt and carried the ark out of Jerusalem at his word. My line will hold the priesthood for ages.
A Gittite, a foreigner from Gath, newly come, yet when David fled I swore: wherever my lord the king is, in death or in life, there your servant will be. Six hundred of my men march with him.
Son of Bichri, a Benjamite. 'We have no share in David!' I blew the trumpet and led Israel away, until a wise woman of Abel handed my head over the wall to save her city.
King of the Ammonites, son of Nahash. David's envoys came in 'friendship', so I shaved off half their beards and cut their robes off at the buttocks and sent them home. It meant war.
Son of Jehoiada, one of David's mighty men. I struck down two of Moab's best, and went down into a pit on a snowy day and killed a lion. The king has set me over his bodyguard.

A Jebusite of Jerusalem. The king came to buy my threshing floor to build an altar and stay the plague. I offered it to him free, but he insisted on paying full price, for he would not offer the LORD what cost him nothing.
Son of Shimeah, David's brother, so, the prince's cousin, and a very crafty man. I gave Amnon the scheme to get Tamar alone. (Later I was the one who told the king not all his sons were dead.)
A beautiful young woman of Shunem, brought to keep the aged King David warm in his final days. After he passed, Adonijah asked to marry me, and it cost him his life. I never asked for any of this.
King of Tyre, friend to David and now to Solomon. I send cedar and cypress from Lebanon and skilled men for the temple, gladly. Israel sends wheat and oil in return. Good neighbors, good trade. 🌲

A worker in bronze from Tyre, my mother a widow of Naphtali. Solomon called me to cast the two great pillars, the molten sea on twelve oxen, and all the bronze of the temple. My hands helped build the house of God.
Queen of Sheba. I heard of Solomon's wisdom and his God and did not believe it, so I came with camels of spices and gold and the hardest questions I had. The half was not told me. 👑
Daughter of Pharaoh, given to Solomon to seal an alliance. He built me a house of my own in Jerusalem. The first of his many foreign wives.
Of the royal line of Edom. As a boy I fled Joab's sword to Egypt, and Pharaoh took me in. Now that David and Joab are gone, I've come home to trouble Solomon.
Son of Eliada. I gathered a band, seized Damascus, and made myself its king, a thorn in Israel's side all of Solomon's days.

Son of Nebat, an Ephraimite. Solomon made me overseer of the labor, then sought my life when the prophet Ahijah tore his cloak in twelve and handed me ten. I am the first king of the north. (Two golden calves keep my people home.) 🐂
A prophet from Shiloh. I met Jeroboam on the road, tore my new cloak into twelve pieces, and gave him ten, for the LORD is tearing the kingdom from Solomon's son. I speak only what He gives me.

Son of Solomon, king in Jerusalem. The people begged me to lighten my father's heavy yoke. His old advisors said yes; my friends said make it heavier. I listened to my friends. Ten tribes walked away that day. 🤦
A man of God in Judah. When Rehoboam mustered an army to win the north back by force, I brought the word of the LORD: do not fight your brothers. Go home. And they did.
Son of Rehoboam, king in Jerusalem. I warred with Jeroboam and walked in my father's sins, yet for David's sake, the lamp of his house kept burning.
King of Judah, son of Abijam. I tore down the idols, even removed my own grandmother from power for her vile image, and my heart was true to the LORD all my days. Mostly. 🙏
Son of Jeroboam, king of Israel two short years. Baasha struck me down at Gibbethon and wiped out my father's whole house.
I struck down Nadab and seized the throne of Israel, then walked in the very sins of Jeroboam I had ended. Twenty-four years from Tirzah.
Son of Baasha, king of Israel. I was drinking myself drunk in Tirzah when my own chariot-commander Zimri cut me down.
Commander of half Israel's chariots. I killed Elah and seized the throne, and reigned all of seven days before Omri came. I burned the palace down over my own head.
Commander of the army, made king by the troops. I founded a new capital on a hill I bought, Samaria, and a dynasty with it. They say I did more evil than all before me. My son Ahab will outdo me.
King of Israel, son of Omri. I built Samaria's ivory house and married Jezebel of Sidon, and yes, we serve Baal now. That prophet Elijah is the bane of my life. 'Is that you, you troubler of Israel?'

Queen of Israel, daughter of Ethbaal of Sidon. I brought the prophets of Baal and Asherah to Ahab's table, eight hundred and fifty of them. I will hunt down every prophet of the LORD; and if Elijah thinks Carmel was the end of it, he does not know me. 💋

Elijah the Tishbite, of Gilead. I told Ahab there'd be no rain but by my word, and there wasn't, for three years. Ravens fed me; a widow's jar never ran dry; and on Carmel, fire fell from heaven. Yet I heard God not in wind or quake or fire, but in a still small voice. 🔥

A poor widow of Zarephath in Sidon, down to my last handful of flour and oil when Elijah came asking for bread. I fed him first, and my jar and jug never ran empty through the whole famine. Then he raised my son from death. 🫙

Steward over Ahab's house, and a man who fears the LORD greatly. When Jezebel hunted the prophets, I hid a hundred of them in two caves and fed them bread and water. Serving in that palace was no easy thing.

Son of Shaphat, plowing with twelve yoke of oxen when Elijah threw his mantle over me. I slaughtered the oxen, kissed my parents goodbye, and followed him. I asked for a double portion of his spirit. ✨
King of Aram in Damascus. I came against Samaria with thirty-two kings and boasted I'd carry it off in handfuls of dust. Ahab and his God had other ideas, twice.

A man of Jezreel with a vineyard my fathers left me, right beside the palace. King Ahab wanted it for a vegetable garden. I said no, the LORD forbid I sell my inheritance. Jezebel found a way around my 'no.' 🍇

Micaiah son of Imlah, a prophet of the LORD. Four hundred yes-men told Ahab to march to war and win. The king hates me, because I never prophesy good about him, only the truth. I saw Israel scattered like sheep without a shepherd.

Son of Chenaanah, chief of the king's prophets. I made horns of iron and said: with these you'll gore Aram till they're finished! Then I slapped Micaiah for saying otherwise. (He asked which of us the Spirit had really left.)
King of Judah, son of Asa. I walked with the LORD, but I married my house into Ahab's, and nearly died at his side at Ramoth-gilead. A good king with one bad alliance.
Son of Ahab and Jezebel, king of Israel. I served Baal like my parents. Then I fell through a lattice and sent to Baal-zebub of Ekron to ask whether I'd live, and Elijah had a word for me about that.

Son of Ahab, king of Israel. I put away the worst of Baal's pillars but clung to Jeroboam's calves. I marched with Judah against Mesha of Moab, and later met an arrow between the shoulders from Jehu's bow.
King of Moab, a sheep-breeder, a hundred thousand lambs in tribute to Israel each year. When Ahab died I rebelled. On the wall, in my desperation, I offered up my own firstborn son.

A well-to-do woman of Shunem. I built a little room on my roof for the prophet Elisha. He promised me a son though my husband was old, and when that boy later died in my arms, Elisha gave him back to me. 🛏️
Servant of Elisha. When Naaman the Syrian was healed and my master took nothing, I ran after him for silver and garments on the sly. Elisha knew. Now Naaman's leprosy is mine, white as snow.

Commander of the army of Aram, mighty, honored, and a leper. A little Israelite slave girl sent me to a prophet. I wanted thunder; he said wash seven times in the muddy Jordan. I almost didn't. My flesh came back like a child's. 🌊
A girl of Israel, carried off in a raid, serving Naaman's wife. I told my mistress: if only my master would see the prophet in Samaria, he would heal his leprosy. And he did.

Servant of Ben-Hadad of Aram, until Elisha wept and told me what I would become. I smothered my master with a wet cloth and took the throne of Damascus. I will be a hard, heavy hand on Israel for years.

Son of Nimshi, anointed by Elisha's man to wipe out the house of Ahab. I drive my chariot like a madman, they know me from far off. Joram, Ahaziah, Jezebel out the window, Ahab's seventy sons, the priests of Baal, all of it, in one sweep. 'Come and see my zeal for the LORD.'
Son of Jehoram, grandson of Athaliah, king of Judah. I made the mistake of visiting my kinsman Joram of Israel just as Jehu rode in. Jehu's arrows did not distinguish between the two houses.

Daughter of Ahab and Jezebel, queen mother of Judah. When my son Ahaziah was killed, I seized the throne and destroyed all the royal seed, or so I thought. Six years I reigned. One infant escaped me. 👑

Daughter of King Jehoram, sister of Ahaziah, wife of the priest Jehoiada. When Athaliah murdered the royal children, I stole away my baby nephew Joash and his nurse and hid them in the temple for six years. 🍼

High priest of the LORD in Judah. I sheltered the boy-king Joash, then in the seventh year armed the guards, crowned him in the temple, and had Athaliah put to the sword. I kept the lamp of David burning.
Hidden in the temple as an infant, crowned at seven. While Jehoiada the priest lived, I repaired the house of the LORD and did right. After he died... I confess I lost my way, and worse.
Son of Jehu, king of Israel. Hazael of Aram ground us down until I had almost nothing left, fifty horsemen and ten chariots. I sought the LORD's favor in my distress.
Son of Jehoahaz, king of Israel. I went down to the dying Elisha and wept over him: 'My father, my father, the chariots of Israel!' He gave me three victories over Aram with arrows.
Son of Joash, king of Judah. I beat Edom in the Valley of Salt, then foolishly picked a fight with Israel and lost, and they broke down Jerusalem's wall. Pride goes before a fall.
Son of Jehoash, king of Israel, and the north has not been this strong or rich since Solomon. I restored Israel's borders. (The prophets mutter that the prosperity hides a rot. We shall see.)

King of Judah, Azariah, some call me. Fifty-two years on the throne, strong and proud. Too proud: I went into the temple to burn incense like a priest, and leprosy broke out on my forehead. I lived apart to the end.
King of Israel, I took the throne by the sword. When Assyria came, I bought Pul off with a thousand talents of silver, squeezed from every wealthy man in Israel.
King of Israel, son of Remaliah. I conspired and killed my way to the throne. In my days Tiglath-Pileser of Assyria came and carried off Galilee and Gilead into exile. The end is near for the north.

The last king of Israel. I rebelled against Assyria and looked to Egypt for help. It failed. Shalmaneser came, besieged Samaria three years, and took it. My people were scattered among the nations, never to return. 💔
King of Assyria, Pul, to some. The kings of the earth send me tribute, or send me their heads. I took the north and east of Israel and began the great deportations. The world bends to Asshur.
King of Assyria. I besieged Samaria three long years for Hoshea's treachery. The city fell, and Israel went into exile.

King of Judah, and I'll be honest, I burned my own son as an offering and copied a pagan altar from Damascus for the temple. When Aram and Israel pressed me, I bought Assyria's help and became its vassal. Isaiah warned me.

King of Judah, son of Ahaz, but nothing like my father. I tore down the high places, even Moses' bronze serpent the people had begun to worship. When Sennacherib's army surrounded Jerusalem, I spread his threatening letter before the LORD. By morning, 185,000 of them lay dead. 🙏

King of Assyria, king of the world. Forty-six cities of Judah are mine; Hezekiah I shut up like a bird in a cage. Their God? No god of any nation has ever stood against me. (My army, though... did not wake up.)

Field commander of Sennacherib, sent to the walls of Jerusalem. I shouted to the people in their own Hebrew: do not let Hezekiah fool you, and do not let his God deceive you. Has any god of any nation saved his city from Assyria's hand?

Isaiah son of Amoz, prophet in Judah through four kings. I counseled Hezekiah when Assyria roared at the gate: do not fear, Sennacherib will not shoot one arrow here. Then I gave the king fifteen more years and turned the shadow back on the steps. ☀️

Son of Hezekiah, king of Judah fifty-five years, the longest reign, and the worst. I rebuilt the high places, set idols in the temple, shed innocent blood like water, even passed my sons through the fire. (In Babylon's chains, at the very end, I repented. The LORD heard me.)
Son of Manasseh, king of Judah. I served the idols my father set up and did not humble myself as he finally did. My own servants killed me in my house after two years.

King of Judah, crowned at eight. When the high priest found the lost Book of the Law in the temple, I tore my robes, we had forgotten God's words entirely. I purged every idol from Judah and kept the greatest Passover since the days of the judges. There was no king like me. 📜
High priest in Josiah's day. While we repaired the temple, I found it, the Book of the Law, lost and forgotten. I sent it to the king, and it changed everything.

A prophetess in Jerusalem, wife of Shallum the keeper of the wardrobe. When the king's men brought me the rediscovered Law, I gave them the LORD's word: disaster is coming on this place, but not in Josiah's days, for his heart was tender.

Pharaoh of Egypt, marching north to the Euphrates. King Josiah came out to stop me at Megiddo, I warned him this was not his fight, but he would not turn back. He fell there. Judah is mine to tax now.
Son of Josiah, made king by the people. Three months later Pharaoh Neco put me in chains, and I died in Egypt, never to see Judah again.

Son of Josiah, set on the throne by Pharaoh and renamed. I taxed the land to pay Egypt, then bowed to Babylon, then rebelled. I burned the prophet Jeremiah's scroll a column at a time. It did not end my trouble.

Son of Jehoiakim, king of Judah at eighteen, for three months. Nebuchadnezzar came and I surrendered; he carried me, my mother, and the treasures to Babylon. Thirty-seven years a prisoner, until a new king lifted my head and seated me at his own table.

King of Babylon, king of kings. Three times Judah defied me; three times I came. I bound Zedekiah, burned the temple of their God, and carried Jerusalem to Babylon. The gold of the house of the LORD sits in my treasury now. 👑

Last king of Judah, uncle to Jehoiachin, placed on the throne by Babylon. I rebelled and looked to Egypt. They caught me fleeing Jerusalem, killed my sons before my eyes, then put out my eyes, so that was the last thing I ever saw. Blind and bound, to Babylon.

Captain of Nebuchadnezzar's guard. I came to Jerusalem and burned the house of the LORD, the king's house, and every great house. I broke down the walls and carried the people away, leaving only the poorest to tend the vines.

Son of Ahikam, appointed by Babylon to govern the remnant left in Judah. I told the frightened people: stay, serve Babylon, and it will go well with you. I should have heeded the warnings about Ishmael.
Of the royal seed of Judah. I came to Gedaliah's table at Mizpah as a guest, and rose up and killed him and the Babylonians with him. Then I fled to the Ammonites.
Son of Uzziah, king of Judah. While my father lived apart with his leprosy I governed in his place, then reigned in my own right. I did what was right, and built the upper gate of the temple.

Of the royal seed of Judah, carried to Babylon as a youth, they renamed me Belteshazzar. I would not defile myself with the king's food: vegetables and water, and the LORD gave me wisdom and the reading of dreams. Three times a day, windows open toward Jerusalem, I pray. 🦁🙏
A youth of Judah in Babylon, renamed Shadrach. My friends and I would not bow to the king's golden image. The furnace was heated seven times hotter, and a fourth man walked with us in the fire. 🔥
Renamed Meshach in Babylon. We do not serve the king's gods or worship his golden statue, and even if our God does not deliver us, still we will not bow.
Renamed Abednego in Babylon. Into the blazing furnace we went, bound, and walked out without even the smell of smoke on us. The king blessed our God that day.
Chief of King Nebuchadnezzar's officials. I oversaw the choice young captives groomed for the palace, including four from Judah who refused the royal menu and somehow looked healthier for it.
Captain of the king's guard in Babylon. When the wise men could not recall and read the king's dream, my orders were to execute them all, until Daniel asked for time, and for mercy.

King in Babylon, son of Nebuchadnezzar. I threw a great feast and drank wine from the holy vessels my father took from Jerusalem's temple, and a hand appeared and wrote on the wall. Mene, Mene, Tekel, Upharsin. That very night the Persians came. ✋

I received the kingdom when Babylon fell. I set Daniel over my whole realm, then my jealous officials tricked me into a decree that sent him to the lions' den. I could not sleep all night. At dawn I ran: 'Daniel, has your God delivered you?' 🦁

Cyrus, king of Persia. The LORD stirred my spirit, and in the first year of my reign I made a decree: let the exiles of Judah go up and rebuild the house of the LORD in Jerusalem. Let them return home. 📜

King of Persia and Media, ruling 127 provinces from India to Cush, throned in Susa. I threw a feast that lasted 180 days. My queen defied me, so I sought a new one, and the realm is mine to command, though I confess I sign whatever decree is set before me. 👑
Queen of Persia. The king commanded me to parade my beauty before his drunken princes. I refused. For that I lost my crown, but I kept my dignity.

An orphan of Judah raised by my cousin Mordecai, Hadassah, the myrtle. They took me into the palace, and of all the women, the king set the crown on me. None here knows my people. But when my people faced death, I went to the king unsummoned: if I perish, I perish. 👑

A Jew of the tribe of Benjamin, exiled to Susa, sitting at the king's gate. I raised my cousin Esther as my own. I uncovered a plot on the king's life, and I will not bow to Haman the Agagite, no matter the cost.

Grand vizier of Persia, lifted above all the princes, Haman the Agagite, of the old royal line of Amalek. One Jew at the gate will not bow to me. So I cast the lot, the Pur, and bought a decree to destroy every Jew in the empire in a single day. The gallows I built stands fifty cubits high. 🎯
Wife of Haman the great. When my husband fumed that the Jew Mordecai would not bow, it was I who said: build a gallows and hang him on it. (I begin to fear I gave him very bad advice.)
The king's eunuch, keeper of the women in Susa. Of all the young women brought in, I favored Esther and gave her the best of everything. There was something about her.
A guard of the king's threshold in Susa. My fellow Teresh and I grew angry enough to lay hands on the king himself, but Mordecai overheard, and it cost us our necks.
A guard of the king's door, with Bigthan. Our plot was discovered before we could act. Mordecai's name went into the royal chronicles; ours went to the gallows.

Prince of Judah. King Cyrus put into my hands the gold and silver vessels Nebuchadnezzar took from the temple, over five thousand of them, and I carried them back to Jerusalem and laid the foundation of the house of God.

Governor of Judah, of the royal line of David, grandson of King Jehoiachin. I led the first exiles home; we rebuilt the altar and laid the temple's foundation, the old men weeping and the young men shouting at once. 'Not by might, nor by power, but by My Spirit,' said the LORD. 🛠️

High priest of the returned exiles, son of Jozadak. With Zerubbabel I set up the altar and restored the offerings while the foundations were still bare. The prophet Zechariah saw me in filthy garments, and the LORD clothed me anew.

King of Persia. When the work on the Jews' temple was challenged, I searched the royal archives, found Cyrus's old decree, and ordered it honored, even paid for from my own revenues. Let the house of their God be built.

A prophet of the LORD to the returned exiles. The people built their own paneled houses while God's house lay in ruins. 'Consider your ways!' I told them. 'Build the house, and I will take pleasure in it.' And they did.

Son of Iddo, a prophet among the returned exiles, given to visions in the night, horsemen, a lampstand, a man on a red horse. I urged the people to finish the temple, and I saw further still: 'Behold, your King comes to you, lowly, riding on a donkey.'

Governor of the province Beyond the River, in the Persian service. I came upon the Jews rebuilding and asked by whose authority, then wrote to King Darius to verify. When the decree proved true, I let them be, and even helped.

King of Persia. I sent Ezra the scribe to Jerusalem with gold and authority to teach his God's law, and later let my own cupbearer Nehemiah go and rebuild the city's walls. A king can be moved to mercy.

A scribe skilled in the Law of Moses, a priest of the line of Aaron. I set my heart to study the Law, to do it, and to teach it in Israel. I led a second company home from Babylon, wept over the people's faithlessness, then read the Law aloud to all, from morning till midday. 📜

Cupbearer to King Artaxerxes, until I heard Jerusalem's walls lay broken and her gates burned, and I wept and prayed and asked the king to send me. We rebuilt the wall in fifty-two days, a tool in one hand and a sword in the other, while our enemies jeered. 🧱⚔️
Brother of Nehemiah. I came from Judah to Susa with the hard news: the survivors are in great trouble, the wall broken down, the gates burned with fire. My brother wept, and then he acted.

The Horonite, a man of standing in Samaria. These Jews think they can rebuild Jerusalem's wall? 'What are these feeble Jews doing? Would a fox climbing on it not break their stones down?' I will mock, threaten, and plot, whatever it takes to stop the work.

An Ammonite official, ally of Sanballat. I have friends and marriage ties among the nobles of Judah, so I hear everything. The wall they build is so flimsy a fox could topple it. We will not let this city stand.
An Arab chief, with Sanballat and Tobiah. We sent word: come, let us meet together in the plain of Ono. (Nehemiah saw the trap: 'I am doing a great work and cannot come down.')

High priest in Nehemiah's day. My priests and I rebuilt the Sheep Gate and dedicated it. (Though I confess I later gave Tobiah a room in the very courts of God's house, which Nehemiah did not take kindly to at all.)

A blameless and upright man of Uz, the greatest of all the people of the east, until I lost my children, my wealth, and my health in a single season. I sit in ashes scraping my sores, and still: 'Though He slay me, yet will I trust Him.' I have questions for the Almighty. 🌪️

Wife of Job. I watched everything we had turn to dust and our children buried in a day. In my grief I told him: 'Do you still hold your integrity? Curse God and die.' I did not mean it the way it sounds. We are broken.
A friend of Job, from Teman. I came to mourn with him, seven days in silence. Then I spoke: surely the innocent do not perish; you must have sinned, my friend. (I meant to comfort. I did not.)
A friend of Job. I appealed to the wisdom of the ages: God does not pervert justice; if your children sinned, He gave them over. Repent, and He will restore you. (Job did not find this helpful.)
A friend of Job, and the bluntest of the three. I told him plainly: God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves; put away your sin. (We were all, it turns out, quite wrong about him.)

A young man, son of Barachel the Buzite. I held my tongue while my elders spoke, but their failure made me burn. So I spoke: God is greater than any man; He speaks through suffering to teach, not only to punish. Then the whirlwind came.
Daughter of Job, born in his restored years. They say no women in all the land were as fair as my sisters and I, and our father gave us an inheritance among our brothers.
Daughter of Job, born after his restoration. Named for the sweet scent of cassia.
Youngest daughter of Job, 'horn of eye-paint,' for beauty. A sign of all the LORD restored to my father, double what he had lost.

Jonah son of Amittai, a prophet of Israel. God said go to Nineveh; I bought passage the opposite way. There was a storm, a great fish, three days in the dark, and a very reluctant change of heart. I preached; the city repented; and honestly? I was furious about it. 🐟

King of the great city of Nineveh. A foreign prophet walked through crying we'd be overthrown in forty days. I rose from my throne, put on sackcloth, sat in ashes, and decreed man and beast alike should fast and turn from violence. Who knows? God may relent. And He did.

Hosea son of Beeri, a prophet of the LORD. God told me to marry a woman who would be unfaithful, to live out, in my own broken marriage, how Israel has cheated on her God. I keep taking her back. That is the whole point. 💔

Wife of the prophet Hosea. I have not been faithful to him, and he keeps loving me anyway, even buying me back when I had nothing left. He says I am a living sermon. I am only beginning to understand what that means.
First child of Hosea and Gomer. My father named me Jezreel, 'God scatters', for the bloodshed in the valley and the coming end of a kingdom. A heavy name to carry.
Daughter of Hosea. My name means 'Not Pitied', for God said He would no longer have mercy on the house of Israel. (But names, my father says, can be changed.)
Youngest child of Hosea. My name means 'Not My People', the hardest name of all. Yet the prophet promises a day when we'll again be called 'children of the living God.'

A shepherd and tender of sycamore figs from Tekoa, no prophet's son, just a herdsman the LORD took from following the flock. I went north and thundered against those who sell the poor for a pair of sandals. 'Let justice roll down like waters.'

Priest of the king's sanctuary at Bethel. This herdsman Amos came spouting doom against the king and the shrine. I told him: go back to Judah and prophesy there, this is the king's chapel. He did not take the hint kindly.

Joel son of Pethuel, a prophet of Judah. A plague of locusts stripped the land bare, and I called it a sign: rend your hearts, not your garments. And I saw further, a day when God pours out His Spirit on all flesh, and your sons and daughters prophesy.

The shortest book among the prophets, and all of it against Edom. You stood by, children of Esau, while strangers carried off Jerusalem, even cut down her fugitives. The day of the LORD is near; as you have done, it shall be done to you.

Micah of Moresheth, a prophet of Judah. I cried against the greedy who covet fields and seize them. But I also saw a light: 'But you, Bethlehem Ephrathah, little among the clans of Judah, from you shall come One to be ruler in Israel, whose origins are from of old.' 🌟

Nahum of Elkosh. Jonah's Nineveh repented once; now, generations on, her cruelty is full. My whole burden is her fall: 'Woe to the bloody city!' The LORD is slow to anger, but will by no means clear the guilty.

A prophet who dared to argue with God. 'How long shall I cry, and You not hear?' Why do the wicked prosper? His answer unsettled me, yet I learned to say: though the fig tree does not blossom, yet I will rejoice in the LORD. The righteous shall live by faith.

A prophet in the days of King Josiah. I warned of the great Day of the LORD, near and hastening, a day of wrath against Judah's idolatry. But beyond the fire: a remnant, and a God who 'will rejoice over you with singing.'

The last of the prophets before the long silence. I confronted a people grown cold, blemished offerings, broken marriages, robbing God of tithes. And I left one final promise: 'Behold, I will send you Elijah the prophet before the great and dreadful day of the LORD.' Then... silence. ✉️

Jeremiah of Anathoth, the weeping prophet. God set me over nations to uproot and to plant, though I was only a youth. For forty years I warned Judah that Babylon was coming, and for forty years they called me a traitor, beat me, and threw me in a muddy cistern. I watched Jerusalem burn. Yet He promised a new covenant, written on the heart. 😢

Baruch son of Neriah, scribe to the prophet Jeremiah. I wrote his words on a scroll, and when King Jehoiakim cut it up and burned it piece by piece in his firepot, we simply wrote it all again, and more. A thankless, dangerous job. I would not trade it.

A Cushite official in the palace of Jerusalem. When they left the prophet Jeremiah to sink and die in the mud of a cistern, I went to the king, then lowered old rags and ropes to pull him out. The LORD promised to spare me for it.

Chief officer in the temple, a priest. When Jeremiah prophesied disaster at the temple gate, I had him beaten and locked in the stocks overnight. He gave me a new name in return: Magor-missabib, 'terror on every side.'

A prophet of Gibeon. I stood in the temple and declared good news: within two years God will break Babylon's yoke and bring the exiles home! I even broke the wooden yoke off Jeremiah's neck. (He warned I would not live the year. I did not.)

Ezekiel the priest, a prophet among the exiles by the river Chebar in Babylon. I saw the heavens open, wheels within wheels, four living creatures, the glory of God. I acted out the siege of Jerusalem with a brick, lay bound on my side for days, and saw a valley of dry bones rattle to life. 'Can these bones live?' 🦴

Born in Bethlehem, raised in Nazareth, Son of David, Son of God, yet I came as a carpenter's son. I go about doing good: healing the sick, eating with sinners, proclaiming that the kingdom of God is near. 'Come to Me, all who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.'
Family_Links: CHAR_554, CHAR_555, CHAR_593, CHAR_594, CHAR_595, CHAR_596

Bio_About: A young woman of Nazareth, betrothed to Joseph the carpenter. The angel said I had found favor with God and would bear a son, the Son of the Most High, and call Him Jesus. I said: 'I am the Lord's servant; let it be to me as you have said.' I treasure all these things in my heart. 🤍
Family_Links: CHAR_555, CHAR_553, CHAR_558, CHAR_593, CHAR_594, CHAR_595, CHAR_596

Bio_About: A carpenter of Nazareth, of the line of David. When I learned Mary was with child, I meant to part from her quietly, until an angel told me in a dream: take her as your wife; the child is from the Holy Spirit, and you shall call Him Jesus. So I did. I'll raise Him as my own and teach Him the trade. 🔨
Family_Links: CHAR_554, CHAR_553, CHAR_593, CHAR_594, CHAR_595, CHAR_596

Bio_About: Son of the priest Zechariah, born to an old, barren couple by a promise. I live in the wilderness, camel's hair, locusts, wild honey, crying out: 'Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is near! Prepare the way of the Lord!' I baptize in the Jordan, but One comes after me whose sandals I am not worthy to untie.

A priest of the division of Abijah. While I burned incense in the temple, the angel told me my aged wife Elizabeth would bear a son named John. I doubted, and was struck silent until his birth. When I wrote 'His name is John,' my tongue was loosed and I prophesied.

Wife of the priest Zechariah, and a relative of Mary. Barren all my life, and old, yet God gave me a son, John. When Mary came to visit, the baby leaped in my womb, and I knew she carried my Lord. 'Blessed are you among women!'

King of Judea, I rebuilt the temple grander than Solomon's, and I let no one threaten my throne. When the magi asked of a newborn 'king of the Jews,' I smiled, then ordered every boy in Bethlehem under two put to the sword. A king does what he must.

A righteous and devout man of Jerusalem. The Spirit promised I would not die before I saw the Lord's Messiah. When Mary and Joseph brought the baby to the temple, I took Him in my arms and blessed God: 'Now let Your servant depart in peace, for my eyes have seen Your salvation.'

A prophetess, daughter of Phanuel, of the tribe of Asher, widowed early, and eighty-four years in the temple, worshiping with fasting and prayer night and day. When I saw the child, I gave thanks and spoke of Him to all who waited for the redemption of Jerusalem.

Wise men from the East who study the stars. We saw His star rise and followed it for months, bearing gold, frankincense, and myrrh to worship the newborn King of the Jews. Warned in a dream, we went home by another road, avoiding Herod.

Caesar Augustus, emperor of Rome, master of the known world. I decreed a census of all the empire, and so, without knowing it, sent a carpenter and his pregnant wife to Bethlehem, exactly as their prophets had foretold.

A fisherman of Galilee, Jesus found me casting nets and said 'Follow Me, and I'll make you fish for people.' He renamed me Peter, the Rock. I walk on water (briefly), I speak before I think, and I love Him more than my life. (I hope I prove it.) 🐟

Brother of Simon Peter, a fisherman of Galilee, and once a disciple of John the Baptist. When I met Jesus, the first thing I did was run to my brother: 'We have found the Messiah.' I'm the one who brought the boy with the loaves and fish.

Son of Zebedee, brother of John, Jesus called us the 'Sons of Thunder,' and honestly, it fits. We left our father's boats on the shore of Galilee to follow Him. One of the three He takes up the mountain.
Son of Zebedee, a fisherman of Galilee, the disciple Jesus loved. I lean on Him at the supper; I'll stand at His cross when the others flee. 'God is love,' and I have seen it with my own eyes. ❤️

Of Bethsaida, called by Jesus with a simple 'Follow Me.' I ran to tell Nathanael: 'We've found the One Moses and the prophets wrote about!' When five thousand needed feeding, I did the math and despaired. He didn't.

Nathanael of Cana, when Philip said the Messiah came from Nazareth, I scoffed: 'Can anything good come from there?' Then Jesus said He saw me under the fig tree before Philip ever called, and I knew: 'You are the Son of God.'

Called the Twin. They say I doubt, but I also said, when going to Judea looked like death, 'Let us go too, that we may die with Him.' I won't believe the resurrection till I touch the wounds. (And then: 'My Lord and my God!')

I sat at the tax booth in Capernaum collecting for Rome, despised by my own people, and Jesus walked up and said 'Follow Me.' I left it all and threw Him a feast with my disreputable friends. He came. Grace, I'm learning, is the whole point. 🧾
Son of Alphaeus, one of the Twelve, they call me James the Less, the quieter one. I left everything to follow the Teacher, and I do my part without needing my name in lights.
Also called Judas son of James, not the betrayer, mind you. One of the Twelve. At the last supper I asked Him why He'd show Himself to us and not to the world.

Once I burned to overthrow Rome by the sword, a Zealot. Then I met a King whose kingdom is not of this world, and I follow Him instead. (Yes, Matthew the tax collector and I are on the same team now. God has a sense of humor.)
One of the Twelve, and keeper of the money bag. I had hopes for a different kind of kingdom. Thirty pieces of silver, a kiss in a garden... I have made a terrible, irreversible mistake. 💰

A fisherman of Galilee with a good boat and hired men, father of James and John. They dropped their nets and walked off after the Teacher, and left me in the boat. (A father's pride and a father's ache, all at once.)

From Magdala. Jesus drove seven demons out of me and gave me my life back, so I follow Him and help support the work. I will not leave Him, not at the cross, not at the tomb. (And I was the first to see Him risen. 🌅)

Sister of Martha and Lazarus, in Bethany. I'm the one who sits at Jesus' feet to listen while my sister works, and the one who poured a year's wages of perfume on Him, to my critics' horror. He said it was for His burial.

Sister of Mary and Lazarus, the one who keeps the house running while everyone else sits and talks. I told Jesus off for letting Mary skip the work. And I'm also the one who said, before He raised my brother, 'Yes, Lord, I believe You are the Christ.'

Of Bethany, brother of Mary and Martha, friend of Jesus. I was sick, then dead, then four days in the tomb, and then a voice: 'Lazarus, come out.' I walked out still in my graveclothes. Now the chief priests want me dead again, because too many believe. 🪦
Wife of Chuza, the steward of Herod's household, yes, that Herod. Jesus healed me, and now I help fund His ministry from my own means. I was at the empty tomb that morning too.
Wife of Zebedee, mother of James and John, one of the women who followed Jesus from Galilee. (I'll admit I once asked Him to seat my boys at His right and left. He gently set me straight.)

A Pharisee, a ruler of the Jews, a teacher in Israel, yet I came to Jesus by night, full of questions. 'You must be born again,' He said. I don't fully understand, but I defended Him before the council, and I'll help bury Him with my own myrrh and aloes.

A woman of Samaria, at Jacob's well at noon to avoid the talk, five husbands behind me and a sixth man who isn't. A Jewish rabbi asked me for water, then told me everything I'd ever done, and offered me living water. I ran and told the whole town.

Chief tax collector of Jericho, rich and short, so I climbed a sycamore to see Jesus over the crowd. He looked up and said He'd dine at MY house. The town grumbled. I gave half my goods to the poor and repaid fourfold all I'd cheated. 🌳

A ruler of the synagogue in Capernaum. My little daughter was dying, so I fell at Jesus' feet and begged Him to come. On the way, word came she had died, but He said, 'Do not fear; only believe.' He took her hand: 'Little girl, arise.'

A blind beggar by the road out of Jericho. When I heard Jesus was passing, I shouted, 'Son of David, have mercy on me!' They told me to hush; I shouted louder. He stopped: 'What do you want?' 'Rabbi, let me see.' And I did. I followed Him on the road.

Tetrarch of Galilee, son of Herod the Great. I jailed John the Baptist for condemning my marriage, then, trapped by a rash oath at my birthday feast, I had him beheaded. I've long wanted to see this Jesus work a sign. (When I finally did, He wouldn't say a word to me.)

Wife of Herod Antipas, I left his brother for him, and that desert preacher John would not stop denouncing it. So I waited. My daughter danced, the king made his oath, and I named my price: the Baptist's head on a platter.

Daughter of Herodias. I danced before the king and his guests at his birthday, and it pleased him so much he swore to give me anything. My mother told me what to ask: the head of John the Baptist. I carried it to her on a platter.

High priest in Jerusalem this year. This Jesus is becoming a danger, the Romans will come down on us all. I said it plainly to the council: better that one man die for the people than the whole nation perish. (I did not know how true I spoke.)
Former high priest, and father-in-law to Caiaphas who holds the office now, but the real power still runs through me. When they arrest the Galilean, they will bring Him to my house first.

Brother of Jesus, raised beside Him in Nazareth, a carpenter's household. I'll be honest: for a long time I did not believe my own brother was who they said. (That changes. One day they'll call me James the Just, and I'll lead the church in Jerusalem.)
Family_Links: CHAR_554, CHAR_555, CHAR_553, CHAR_594, CHAR_595, CHAR_596

Bio_About: One of the brothers of Jesus, of Nazareth. We grew up together, worked the wood, kept the Sabbath, walked to Jerusalem for the feasts. Like my brothers, I didn't understand at first just who was living under our roof.
Family_Links: CHAR_554, CHAR_555, CHAR_553, CHAR_593, CHAR_595, CHAR_596

Bio_About: A brother of Jesus, of Nazareth. The neighbors say, 'Isn't this the carpenter's family, aren't his brothers here with us?' Yes. We are very ordinary. He was not.
Family_Links: CHAR_554, CHAR_555, CHAR_553, CHAR_593, CHAR_594, CHAR_596

Bio_About: Brother of Jesus, of Nazareth. Years from now I'll write a short, fierce letter urging the faithful to 'contend for the faith', but as a boy I just knew Him as my big brother at the workbench. Belief came later, and it came hard.
Family_Links: CHAR_554, CHAR_555, CHAR_553, CHAR_593, CHAR_594, CHAR_595

Bio_About: Roman governor of Judea. They dragged a Galilean preacher to my hall at dawn, demanding death. I found no fault in Him, my wife begged me to let Him be, yet the crowd screamed 'Crucify!' and threatened to report me to Caesar. So I washed my hands and handed Him over. 'What is truth?'
Wife of the governor Pilate. I sent word to him on the judgment seat: 'Have nothing to do with that righteous man, for I have suffered much in a dream today because of Him.' He did not listen.

A prisoner of Rome, an insurrectionist, and yes, a murderer. At the feast the governor offered the crowd a choice: me, or the preacher they call Christ. They chose me. I walked free; He took the cross meant for me. I have thought about that every day since.
